name: The Elemental Cleric
concept: You serve one of the raw elements — fire, earth, air, water, silt, or magma — not a god who wears a face and hears prayers kindly, but the vast thing itself. It is patient, and it does not love you. It bargains. And in a dying world, its gifts are the difference between a people who live and a people who become dust.
duty: The element gives, and the element demands. Keep its rites and its taboos, or find out what it is like to be forsaken by the very ground or sky.

look.label: Mark
options: a vessel of it always carried
scars from a rite gone hard

look.label: Dress
options: ash, salt, or dust ground in for good

gear.label: Charge
options: a taboo you must never break
a debt of Favor already owed

moves.name: Elemental Rite
moves.trigger: When you call on your element under pressure and offer it something that matters.

results.success: The element answers with a sign or aid true to its nature.
results.failure: Only silence, or an answer you did not want; the GM makes a move.
advantage: Hold 1 Favor (spend later per core.md: bless a roll that serves the element, ask an omen, turn a weak result stronger, ward off a minor spiritual harm, or declare a shrine or sign is near).
The aid is greater or lasts longer than you asked.
threat: The element demands visible obedience or a further offering, now.
A rival power feels you draw on the deep force.
results.triumph: A surge of elemental power, freely given.
results.despair: You have offended the element. Mark Wrath.

hold.name: Favor
spend: Add a boost die to a roll that clearly serves the element's nature.
Ask the GM for an omen or sign.
Turn a bare success on a holy act into a strong one.
Ward an ally from a minor curse, fear, or spiritual harm.
Declare a shrine, spring, ember, or sign of your element is near.

moves.name: Sworn to the Element
moves.trigger: Because you have given yourself to the element (core.md: Pray for Aid, Devotion, Wrath):
moves.text: You can perform its rites and seek its aid, and you gain Devotion by upholding its way at cost. But its taboos bind you absolutely; break one and mark Wrath. The element is not a friend. It is a force you have learned to bargain with.

moves.name: Shape the Element
moves.trigger: When you command your element where it is present in the scene.

results.success: It moves to your will: the fire leaps, the water gathers, the earth shifts, the wind rises.
results.failure: It will not heed you here, or it heeds too well.
advantage: Shape it larger, or to two ends at once.
Do it without spending from your Favor.
threat: The element exacts a small toll for the command.
It does as you say and a little more.
results.triumph: You command it as though you were part of it.
results.despair: The element turns your command back on you.

moves.name: Elemental Sense
moves.trigger: When you reach out to feel your element in the land around you.

results.success: You sense it: where the water hides, where the fire will spread, where the ground is hollow or the wind will turn.
results.failure: The element is silent to you here, and the silence itself may mean something.
advantage: Sense it far off, or in fine detail.
Feel another power that is near it.
threat: Reaching out, you brush something that reaches back.
results.triumph: You feel the whole shape of your element across the region.
results.despair: You sense your element being defiled, and it recoils through you.

moves.name: Cleanse or Scour
moves.trigger: When you turn your element to heal and purify, or to burn and scour away.

results.success: Wounds close, poison and rot are washed out, or corruption is burned clean — as fits your element.
results.failure: It is beyond your element's power here, or the cure costs as much as the ill.
advantage: Leave a lasting blessing on the place or person.
threat: The rite draws on you: setback until you rest.
Spend 1 Favor or mark a small Wrath to finish it.
results.triumph: A cleansing so complete it becomes a sign the Legion will speak of.
results.despair: You scour away more than the sickness.

bonds: ____ has kept faith with my element beside me, and I trust their hands at the rite.
____ mocks the element, and I fear the day it decides to answer them.
I owe ____ a healing I could not give, and it sits between us.
____ broke one of my element's taboos to save my life, and I have not told them what it may cost.
____ is the one I would spend my last Favor to protect.
communityTie: The Free Legion lives or dies by water and weather; ____ knows your rites are why they still live.
The Legion prays to you as much as to the element; ____ warns you not to let them confuse the two.
Your element has laid a claim on the Legion's path; ____ helps you keep its taboos without the others knowing why.

tracks.name: Wrath
tracks.note: The element's displeasure (core.md). It does not forgive lightly.

basic: Take a move from another playbook.
Take a move from another playbook.
Deepen a rite: name a greater working of your element you can attempt at Daunting difficulty.
Raise your Devotion by 1 through a rite or sacrifice that costs you.
Train a move you lean on: it upgrades 2 dice to proficiency (never more than its stat).
Make peace with the element: reduce your Wrath by 1 through pilgrimage or restitution.
advanced: Raise one stat by 1 (max 5). Rare, and only at a true turning point.
Become a Chosen: the element marks you as its instrument, with all the danger that carries.
Raise a shrine: found a Bastion (a holy place of your element) for the Legion to gather around.
